methodology_note: No headless browser/font-rendering engine was available in this environment, so text bounding boxes are estimated analytically from the declared font metrics rather than measured from rasterized output: width = (avg glyph width factor x font-size x char count) + letter-spacing x (char count - 1), where avg glyph width factor = 0.55 for both the serif and sans stacks used here (a standard approximation for mixed-case Latin text at these weights). Vertical extent uses ascent = 0.8 x font-size above the baseline and a conservative descent = 0.25 x font-size below the baseline for any text containing lowercase letters (applied even when the specific glyphs used may lack descenders, to avoid under-counting), or 0.05 x font-size for all-caps/tabular text. All coordinates below are otherwise exact, since they come directly from the SVG source (rects, lines, and text x/y attributes), not estimates.

svg_parse.notes: Well-formed XML, single root <svg>, viewBox present, no external href/@import/script references.

overlap_checks.note: microcopy occupies bottom-left (x 80-396.8), CTA occupies bottom-right (x 860-1120); horizontally disjoint
cta-text bbox [895.55,522.4,1084.45,541.1] is fully inside cta-button bbox [860,500,1120,564] with >=22px padding on every side
disjoint on both axes: mark is at y 582-588 (below cta-button bottom 564) and x 576-624 (left of cta-button left edge 860)

clipping_checks.note: right edge at x=1120, 80px inside canvas right edge (1200) and 56px inside frame right edge (1176)
widest text element, right edge at x=678.4, well inside content right margin (1120) and canvas (1200)
bottom edge at y=588, inside frame bottom (606) and canvas (630)
maximum computed text right-edge across all rows is 1084.45 (cta-text); minimum left-edge is 80 (content margin); no bbox exceeds [0,1200] horizontally or [0,630] vertically

contrast_checks.defect: ink.muted token is far below both the normal-text (4.5:1) and large-text (3:1) AA thresholds; text is legible only under high ambient contrast and fails accessibility gate.

unreadable_text_check.note: No text is clipped, rotated, or overset. However, the 5 text nodes rendered in ink.muted are effectively unreadable in normal viewing conditions due to the contrast failure above; readability is coupled to the contrast defect rather than a separate geometric issue.

overall_summary: Loop-01 establishes a coherent editorial grid with no geometric defects (no overlap, no clipping, all required content present, minimum font size 16px), but the ink.muted caption/label color fails WCAG AA contrast against the paper background (2.49:1 vs. 4.5:1 required), affecting 5 text nodes: the three metadata labels, the edition tag, and the microcopy line. This is the single objective defect carried into loop-02.
